records.ety: [L. indifferentia similarity, lack of difference: cf. F. indiffrence.]

defs: The quality or state of being indifferent, or not making a difference; lack of sufficient importance to constitute a difference; absence of weight; insignificance.
Impartiality; freedom from prejudice, prepossession, or bias.
quotes: He . . . is far from such indifference and equity as ought and must be in judges which he saith I assign.

defs: Absence of anxiety or interest in respect to what is presented to the mind; unconcernedness; as, entire indifference to all that occurs.
quotes: Indifference can not but be criminal, when it is conversant about objects which are so far from being of an indifferent nature, that they are highest importance.
